verse_text: and the priest shall bring these things as a deposit offering before the Lord; they are holy for the priest, together with the breast of the deposit offering and the thigh of the choice portion. After that, the one vowing may drink wine.'

links.context: The vow Paul makes is likely a form of the Nazirite vow ([[NUM.6:1]]-21) given in thanksgiving for deliverance from danger.

Not only did the people apostatize, the Nazirites became blacker than soot. The Nazirites ([[NUM.6:2]], [[NUM.6:19]], [[NUM.6:20]]) were the "monks" of Israel who never cut their hair, refused wine, and devoted themselves entirely to God. By this we see that Israel was utterly desolate.

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden
